---
title: "Georeferenciación"
author: "GJBM"
format: html
editor: visual
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---
# Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}

packages <- c("tidyverse", "readxl", "readr", "data.table", "dplyr", "stringr", "ggplot2", "sf", "fs", "rnaturalearth", "rnaturalearthdata", "rio", "countrycode","maps", "stringi","rnaturalearthdata","devtools", "pack", "cli", "remotes", "countrycode","progress", "tidygeocoder", "dplyr", "readr", "purrr","ggplot")

# Install packages not yet installed [for the first time]
installed_packages <- packages %in% rownames(installed.packages())
if (any(installed_packages == FALSE)) {
 install.packages(packages[!installed_packages])
}

# Packages loading
invisible(lapply(packages, require, character.only = TRUE)) # remove "invisible" to see whether the packages are loaded/available. 

Sys.setlocale("LC_ALL", "Spanish_Spain.1252") # en Windows
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}

dfAssisDiagonal <- read.csv2("./data/Georreferencing/1.Raw/georef__assis_i_2026_diagonal.csv",sep = ",")

names(dfAs) # check names of the columns
View(dfAssisDiagonal)
# if you want to open and see the file
str(dfAssisDiagonal) #solo
headdfAssisDiagonalhead(dfCR) # if you want to see more or less the structure of the dataframe (df)

```

```{r}
unique(dfAssisDiagonal$country) #ok
unique(dfAssisDiagonal$stateProvince)
unique(dfAssisDiagonal$locality)
names(dfAssisDiagonal)
```

```{r}
library(sf)
library(rnaturalearth)

countries_sf <- ne_countries(scale = 10, returnclass = "sf") %>%
  st_make_valid() %>%
  st_transform(4326) %>%
  select(country_rev = admin, countryCode_rev = iso_a2)

pts <- dfAssisDiagonal %>% 
  filter(!is.na(decimalLatitude), !is.na(decimalLongitude)) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude"), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE)

pts_joined <- st_join(pts, countries_sf, join = st_within, left = TRUE) %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(.row_id, country_rev, countryCode_rev)

db <- db %>%
  left_join(pts_joined, by = ".row_id") %>%
  mutate(
    country     = coalesce(country,     country_rev),
    countryCode = coalesce(countryCode, countryCode_rev),
    changes = append_change_tag(
      changes,
      paste0("country/countryCode filled from reverse geocoding using ne_countries [",
             Sys.Date(), "]"))
  ) %>%
  select(-country_rev, -countryCode_rev)
```

```{r}
if (!"ok.2" %in% names(dfAssisDiagonal)) dfAssisDiagonal$ok.2 <- dfAssisDiagonal$.ok_for_analysis
if (!"discardReason" %in% names(dfAssisDiagonal)) dfAssisDiagonal$discardReason <- NA_character_
view(dfAssisDiagonal)

code <- dfAssisDiagonal$occurrenceID
code

# make sure ok.2 exists
if (!"ok.2" %in% names(df11)) df11$ok.2 <- df11$.ok_for_analysis

# make sure discardReason exists
if (!"discardReason" %in% names(df11)) df11$discardReason <- NA_character_

# CORRER AL FINAL
for (code in code) {
  idx <- which(df11$occurrenceID == code)
  if (length(idx)) {
    dfAssisDiagonal$ok.2[idx] <- TRUE
    dfAssisDiagonal$discardReason[idx] <- "Georeference using inverse geocoding"
  }
}

# CORRER AL FINAL 
dfAssisDiagonal$coordinateUncertaintyInMeters<-0
# dfAssisDiagonal$ok.2[73] <- T
```

```{r}
## ============================================================
## GEOCODIFICACIÓN INVERSA - Nominatim (OpenStreetMap)
## Extrae locality y stateProvince a partir de decimalLatitude/decimalLongitude
## ============================================================

library(httr)
library(jsonlite)

# ---- 1. Cargar tus datos ----
# Cambia la ruta por la de tu archivo
# df <- read.csv("tus_datos.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE, encoding = "UTF-8")

# Columnas nuevas para no sobreescribir de inmediato tus originales
# (así puedes revisar antes de reemplazar stateProvince y locality)
dfAssisDiagonal$locality_geo      <- NA_character_
dfAssisDiagonal$stateProvince_geo <- NA_character_
dfAssisDiagonal$country_geo       <- NA_character_

# ---- 2. Función auxiliar: toma el primer campo no-nulo disponible ----
get_field <- function(addr, fields) {
  for (f in fields) {
    if (!is.null(addr[[f]]) && nzchar(addr[[f]])) return(addr[[f]])
  }
  return(NA_character_)
}

# ---- 3. Función de geocodificación inversa contra Nominatim ----
reverse_geocode_nominatim <- function(lat, lon) {
  url <- "https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/reverse"

  res <- tryCatch({
    GET(
      url,
      query = list(
        lat = lat,
        lon = lon,
        format = "json",
        addressdetails = 1,
        zoom = 10   # 10 = nivel ciudad/localidad; sube a 14 si quieres más detalle (barrio)
      ),
      # Nominatim EXIGE un User-Agent identificable con contacto real
      user_agent("R-reverse-geocoding-script (tu_email@ejemplo.com)")
    )
  }, error = function(e) NULL)

  if (is.null(res) || httr::status_code(res) != 200) return(NULL)

  parsed <- tryCatch(
    fromJSON(content(res, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8")),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  return(parsed)
}

# ---- 4. Loop principal ----
# Guarda progreso cada 50 filas por si se corta la conexión
archivo_progreso <- "progreso_geocoding.rds"

# Si ya existe un progreso guardado, retómalo
if (file.exists(archivo_progreso)) {
  df <- readRDS(archivo_progreso)
  cat("Progreso previo cargado.\n")
}

for (i in seq_len(nrow(dfAssisDiagonal))) {

  # Saltar filas ya procesadas (útil si se reanuda el script)
  if (!is.na(dfAssisDiagonal$locality_geo[i])) next

  lat <- dfAssisDiagonal$decimalLatitude[i]
  lon <- dfAssisDiagonal$decimalLongitude[i]

  if (is.na(lat) || is.na(lon)) next

  result <- reverse_geocode_nominatim(lat, lon)

  if (!is.null(result) && !is.null(result$address)) {
    addr <- result$address

    # locality: intenta varios niveles administrativos en orden de especificidad
    dfAssisDiagonal$locality_geo[i] <- get_field(
      addr, c("city", "town", "village", "municipality", "county", "suburb")
    )

    # stateProvince: nivel estado/provincia/departamento
    dfAssisDiagonal$stateProvince_geo[i] <- get_field(addr, c("state", "region"))

    # país, útil para verificar que caiga en Brazil/Paraguay/Argentina/Bolivia
    dfAssisDiagonal$country_geo[i] <- get_field(addr, c("country"))
  }

  # Respeta el límite de uso de Nominatim: máx. 1 solicitud/segundo
  Sys.sleep(1)

  if (i %% 50 == 0) {
    cat("Procesadas", i, "de", nrow(dfAssisDiagonal), "coordenadas\n")
    saveRDS(dfAssisDiagonal, archivo_progreso)
  }
}

# ---- 5. Guardar resultado final ----
# saveRDS(dfAssisDiagonal, archivo_progreso)
# write.csv(dfAssisDiagonal, "datos_geocodificados.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# ---- 6. Revisión rápida ----
table(dfAssisDiagonal$country_geo, useNA = "ifany")
sum(is.na(dfAssisDiagonal$locality_geo))       # cuántas quedaron sin localidad
sum(is.na(dfAssisDiagonal$stateProvince_geo))  # cuántas quedaron sin provincia/estado

# ---- 7. (Opcional) Reemplazar tus columnas originales solo donde estaban vacías ----
# df$locality[is.na(df$locality) | df$locality == ""] <-
#   df$locality_geo[is.na(df$locality) | df$locality == ""]
#
# df$stateProvince[is.na(df$stateProvince) | df$stateProvince == ""] <-
#   df$stateProvince_geo[is.na(df$stateProvince) | df$stateProvince == ""]


```

```{r}
limpiar_texto <- function(texto) {
  texto_limpio <- iconv(texto, to = "ASCII//TRANSLIT")
  return(texto_limpio)
}

dfAssisDiagonal$stateProvince <- limpiar_texto(dfAssisDiagonal$stateProvince)

dfAssisDiagonal$locality <- limpiar_texto(dfAssisDiagonal$locality)
```


```{r}
# CORRER AL FINAL
write.csv(dfAssisDiagonal, file = "C:/Users/gelar/Desktop/X/AssisDiagonal/AssisDiagonal.csv")

```

```{r}
dfAssisDiagonalCorregido <- read.csv2("C:/Users/gelar/Desktop/X/AssisDiagonal/AssisDiagonal.csv",sep = ";")
str(dfAssisDiagonalCorregido)
str(dfAssisDiagonal)
```

```{r}
dfAssisDiagonal <- dfAssisDiagonal %>%

  left_join(
    dfAssisDiagonalCorregido %>% select(
      occurrenceID, 
      locality_geo1 = locality_geo, 
      stateProvince_geo1 = stateProvince_geo
    ),
    by = "occurrenceID"
  ) %>%
  

  mutate(
    locality = coalesce(locality_geo1, locality_geo),
    stateProvince = coalesce(stateProvince_geo1, stateProvince_geo)
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Eliminamos las columnas temporales para dejar la base de datos limpia
  select(-locality_geo1, -stateProvince_geo1)
dfAssisDiagonal$ok.2 <- T
dfAssisDiagonal$discardReason <- "Inverse Geocoding, all localities and stateProvince where completed"
```

```{r}
write.csv(dfAssisDiagonal, file = "C:/Users/gelar/Desktop/Voluntariado/EpIG_volunteership/data/Georreferencing/2.Processed/georef__georef__assis_i_2026_diagonal.csv")

```

#Jimenez_d_2023
```{r}
dfAssisDiagonal <- read.csv2("./data/Georreferencing/1.Raw/georef__assis_i_2026_diagonal.csv",sep = ",")

names(dfAs) # check names of the columns
View(dfAssisDiagonal)
# if you want to open and see the file
str(dfAssisDiagonal) #solo
headdfAssisDiagonalhead(dfCR) # if you want to see more or less the structure of the dataframe (df)

```
